skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI image prompts and run inference.sh image apps for text-to-image, image editing, upscaling, stitching, and model discovery.
skill.summary: Use inference.sh image models for generation, editing, upscaling, and related image workflows.
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirms the skill depends on the inference.sh CLI and network-hosted image apps, while most Ruby backtick findings are Markdown code spans or fenced command examples rather than executable Ruby. The install command pipes a remote script into sh, which is a real supply-chain risk, but no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden persistence was found.

medium_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
medium_findings.description: The quick start recommends curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh. Piping a downloaded script directly to sh can execute changed or compromised remote code before the user reviews it. The later install note describes checksum verification, but the quick start still presents a risky default path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell install pattern is present in the quick start. It is risky even when the referenced service is legitimate because execution happens before local inspection.

medium_findings.title: External CLI and Network Service Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run infsh app commands against hosted image applications and remote image URLs. This is expected for the stated purpose, but users should understand prompts and image URLs may be sent to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and examples directly invoke the inference.sh CLI and include remote image URLs. This matches the skill purpose, so it is a transparency and data-handling concern rather than evidence of abuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh pages, a hosted image, CLI distribution endpoints, documentation, and placeholder image URLs. These URLs are expected for documentation and service use, but they indicate external network dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URLs are visible in documentation and examples. They are not hidden exfiltration endpoints, but they confirm that the workflow depends on network services.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static Ruby/shell backtick detections map to Markdown code fences, inline app identifiers, and command examples in SKILL.md. No Ruby code or dynamic shell interpolation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown examples and inline code spans, not executable Ruby source. The only actual execution risk is the user-invoked CLI guidance.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at SKILL.md line 3 appears to match descriptive text about image models, not cryptographic code. The only checksum reference found is SHA-256 in the install note, which is not weak.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter text describing models and triggers. No hashing implementation or weak algorithm use is present there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start recommends curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh. Piping a downloaded script directly to sh can execute changed or compromised remote code before the user reviews it. The later install note describes checksum verification, but the quick start still presents a risky default path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell install pattern is present in the quick start. It is risky even when the referenced service is legitimate because execution happens before local inspection.

content.value_statement: Creative teams need fast access to multiple image models without rewriting commands for each provider. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inference.sh CLI workflows for generation, editing, upscaling, and model discovery.
actual_capabilities: Runs inference.sh image apps from prompts using documented infsh commands.
Lists available image applications through the inference.sh CLI.
Shows examples for FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and Topaz workflows.
Supports prompt patterns for text-to-image, image editing, text rendering, and upscaling.
Provides command examples for stitching multiple remote images.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running image applications.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI, account access, and any provider-side permissions.
Sends prompts, parameters, and remote image URLs to external inference.sh services.
Does not include local image generation models or offline execution.
Model availability, pricing, and output quality depend on inference.sh and upstream providers.

use_cases.title: Create campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Generate product scenes, social posts, and marketing concepts from clear prompt briefs.

use_cases.title: Explore concept art directions
use_cases.description: Compare multiple hosted image models for characters, environments, posters, and style tests.

use_cases.title: Automate image production tasks
use_cases.description: Use CLI commands for repeatable generation, upscaling, image stitching, and documentation examples.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to generate an image of [subject] in [style]. Use a square aspect ratio and suggest a concise prompt first.

prompt_templates.title: Compare model options
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose an image model for a task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend an inference.sh image model for [goal]. Explain the tradeoffs, then prepare the infsh command.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a production prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a production-ready prompt for [brand or product]. Include lighting, composition, background, format, and negative constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Build a multi-step workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a CLI workflow that generates [image type], upscales the result, and stitches selected outputs into one final asset.

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of a ceramic coffee mug for an online store.
output_examples.output: A concise FLUX prompt focused on studio lighting, clean background, product scale, and ecommerce presentation, plus the matching inference.sh command to run it.

output_examples.input: Make a poster with readable headline text for an event.
output_examples.output: A Reve-oriented prompt that specifies the exact headline, layout, contrast, and poster style, with guidance to review text accuracy after generation.

output_examples.input: Upscale a finished image and combine it with another reference image.
output_examples.output: A two-step workflow that uses the Topaz upscaler first, then the stitch-images app with selected image URLs and direction settings.

best_practices: Review install scripts manually and verify checksums before running them.
Use precise prompts that define subject, style, composition, lighting, and output format.
Avoid sending confidential images, private prompts, or unreleased campaign details to external services.
anti_patterns: Do not pipe remote install scripts directly to a shell in sensitive environments.
Do not assume every hosted model has the same parameters, limits, or content policy.
Do not use vague prompts when brand, layout, or text accuracy matters.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh CLI to run hosted image applications such as FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and Topaz.

faq.question: Does it run models locally?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflows call external inference.sh services through the infsh command-line tool.

faq.question: Can it edit or upscale images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for image upscaling, image stitching, and model workflows suited to editing tasks.

faq.question: Is an inference.sh account required?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start includes infsh login, so users need valid access before running hosted applications.

faq.question: Is the install command safe?
faq.answer: The quick start uses a remote script piped to sh. Review the script and verify checksums before installing the CLI.

faq.question: What should not be sent through this skill?
faq.answer: Do not send confidential prompts, private image URLs, unreleased product details, or data that should not leave your environment.
